title: Get your NY State ID
instructions: Bring your birth certificate and proof of release to a NYC DMV office. Fill out Form MV-44. If you have proof of public assistance, you can request a fee waiver.
what_to_bring: Birth certificate, proof of release papers, proof of NY address (shelter letter or mail). Application form MV-44.

title: Open a bank account
instructions: Once you have your state ID, you can open a free or low-cost bank account so paychecks and benefits have a place to land.
where_to_go: Any major bank branch in Brooklyn
what_to_bring: NY State ID, Social Security card, small opening deposit (some accounts have no minimum).

title: Apply for transitional housing (Fortune Society Castle)
instructions: Call The Fortune Society for an intake screening. Be ready to share your release date and current shelter situation.
what_to_bring: Have your state ID, release papers, and any treatment or programming records available.

title: Apply for SNAP and Medicaid
instructions: Use ACCESS HRA online to apply for both at once. Phone interview within 1–2 weeks.
what_to_bring: State ID, Social Security number, proof of release date.

title: Start work with Center for Employment Opportunities
instructions: Walk in Mon-Fri at 8am for CEO orientation. You can start on a paid day-labor crew within the week while CEO helps find you a permanent job.
what_to_bring: State ID, comfortable work clothes and shoes.
